---
title: "Politics in Federal Agency Policymaking"
date: today
title-slide-attributes:
    data-background-image: ""
    data-background-size: cover
    data-background-opacity: "0.5"
format:
 revealjs:
   footer: ''
   incremental: false   
   smaller: false
   scrollable: true
   theme: [../michigan.scss]
css: styles.css
execute:
  echo: false
  engine: knitr
embed-resources: true
---


```{r knit-options}
library(scales) # for percents
library(here)
library(tidyverse)

# inline numbers round to 2, comma at thousands
knitr::knit_hooks$set(inline = function(x) {
  if (is.na(as.numeric(x))) {
    return (x)
    } else
        return (as.numeric(x) |> 
                 round(2) |>
                 format(big.mark=",") 
        )
})

d <- "pressure-book/"
```

## Today

1. A note on rulemaking in the 2nd Trump administration 
2. *Public Pressure in Bureaucratic Policymaking* book proposal

## The public narrative 

::: {layout="[[1, 1]]"}
![](../figs/NYT_Words_Disappear.jpg "Banned Words")

![](../figs/nyt-eo-100.png "Executive orders by president")
:::

## NYT articles about agencies using [racialized terms](#terms)

::: {layout="[[1, 1]]"}
![](../../racialization/docs/figs/trends-nyt-total-1.png)


![](../../racialization/docs/figs/trends-nyt-total-2.png)
:::

## {data-menu-title="Race in Policy Documents"}

![](../../racialization/docs/figs/trends-rule-docs-2.png)


## {data-menu-title="Civil Rights"}

![](../../racialization/docs/figs/trends-rule-docs-civil-rights-2.png)

<!---
## {data-menu-title="DEI"}


![](../../racialization/docs/figs/trends-rules-civil-rights-2.png)
--> 

## {data-menu-title="African Americans"}

![](../../racialization/docs/figs/trends-rule-docs-groups-5.png)

## {data-menu-title="Alaska Native"}

![](../../racialization/docs/figs/trends-rule-docs-groups-11.png)

## {data-menu-title="Accessiblity"}

![](../../racialization/docs/figs/trends-rule-docs-all-term-3.png)

## {data-menu-title="Climate Change"}

![](../../racialization/docs/figs/trends-rule-docs-all-term-92.png)

## {data-menu-title="Climate Change"}

![](../../racialization/docs/figs/trends-rule-docs-all-term-172.png)

## {data-menu-title="Evidence-based"}

![](../../racialization/docs/figs/trends-rule-docs-all-term-191.png)


## {data-menu-title="Rules"}

58% fewer rules, 56% fewer other documents vs 2017

::: {layout="[[1.6, 1]]"}

![](../../racialization/docs/figs/rulemaking-2.png)


- Capacity hit (e.g., DOGE)?
- Litigation risk of writing things down? 
- Unitary executive principled rejection of process? 
- Keeping heads down?

:::

##  {data-menu-title="by agency"}

58% fewer rules, 56% fewer other documents vs 2017

::: {layout="[[1.6, 1]]"}

![](../../racialization/docs/figs/rulemaking-7.png)


- Capacity hit (e.g., DOGE)?
- Litigation risk of writing things down? 
- Unitary executive principled rejection of process? 
- Keeping heads down?

:::


<!--
|.        | Rules | Other Documents |
|:--------|:------------|:-----------|
|DOI      |-2%          |-18%        |
|DHS      |-20%         |-36%        |
|DOE      |-39%         |-70%        |
|HHS      |-39%         |+32%         |
|DOT      |-69%         |-35%        |
|DOC      |-75%         |-64%        |
|USDA     |-75%         |-69%        |
|FCC      |-76%         |-74%        |
|EPA      |-78%         |-56%        |
|Treasury |-80%         |-40%        |
|DOD      |-81%         |-55%        |
--> 

::: {.notes}
BLM is up 300%-500% in final rules

DOE up 150% in final rules 

CFTC, DOD, HUD, SEC, FEC down to 0 

EPA is down 81% in rules, 58% in other documents 

DHS is down 42% IN RULES 57% IN OTHER DOCUMENTS 

DOL is down less 

DOJ is down 36% in final rules, down in drafts, but up in other documents

ED is down 85% in rules
:::

## {data-menu-title="boxplots"}

::: {layout="[[1, 1,1,1]]"}



![❌ Targeted by DOGE](../../racialization/docs/figs/rulemaking-change-doge-1.png)



![❌Percieved Agency Ideology](../../racialization/docs/figs/rulemaking-change-box-2025-2.png)

![❌Percieved Agency Centrism](../../racialization/docs/figs/rulemaking-change-box-2025-3.png)

![✔️More attention in Project 2025](../../racialization/docs/figs/rulemaking-change-box-2025-7.png)

<!--
![❌Racialized in NYT](../../racialization/docs/figs/rulemaking-change-box-2025-4.png)


![? Racialized in Project 2025](../../racialization/docs/figs/rulemaking-change-box-2025-5.png)

![? Historically Racialized in Rules](../../racialization/docs/figs/rulemaking-change-box-2025-1.png)
--> 


:::

## Democracy beyond voting

1. *Public Pressure in Bureaucratic Policymaking* 
2. *Interbranch Representation: How congressional offices represent constituencies to the bureaucracy*


## Public pressure in policymaking for realists

How to think about "participation" in agency rulemaking

. . . 

**Who:** Organized groups, not individuals

. . . 

**Mechanisms:** Technical & legal information, but also

- political information 
- issue framing

. . . 

**Effects** on policy (1) *language* & (2) *substance*

. . . 

**Conditional on** capacity and procedures  --- i.e., it is it someone's job inside the agency? 
(not strategic behavior)

# **Who:** Organized groups, not individuals

## Where we go wrong 

::: {layout="[[1, 1]]"}

![Freedom of Speech by Norman Rockwell](https://rockwellfourfreedoms.org/wp-content/uploads/2018/03/FreedomofSpeech-sm-2.jpg "Freedom of Speech by Norman Rockwell")

- methodological individualism (not independent units of analysis)
- projecting a small democracy ideal (deliberation) onto large democracy politics

:::

## Organized groups, not individuals

::: {layout="[[1, 1]]"}
mass comments 

table 
:::

## Why this matters for scholars

balla paper

## Why this matters for advocates and officials

"personalized" comments 

$\rightarrow$ torrents of useless email, pretending not to be organized by groups but are less informative and likely end up in the trash 

$\rightarrow$ increase costs of participation in petitioning


# **What:** Technical and legal information

## Technical and legal information

```{r}
here("FINREGRULEMAKE2", 
     "finreg", 
     "figs", 
     "boxplot-efficacyXsophistication-2.png") |> 
  str_remove(d) |> 
  knitr::include_graphics()
```


## Agency rulemaking tilts toward the rich

```{r}
here("FINREGRULEMAKE2", 
     "finreg", 
     "figs", 
     "assets-efficacy-6.png") |> 
  str_remove(d) |> 
knitr::include_graphics()
```


## Why? 

### $\rightarrow$ Big companies hire experts and lawyers

```{r, out.width= "100%"}
here("FINREGRULEMAKE2", 
     "finreg", 
     "figs", 
     #"mediation-4way-acme-1.png"
     "mediation-4wayNo0-acme-1.png"
     ) |> 
  str_remove(d) |> 
knitr::include_graphics()
```




# **What:** Political information 

Mass commenting campaigns is petitioning, which demonstrates quantitative relevance and mobilizes allies.

## Coalition size

# **What:** Issue framing

## Issue framing affects policy

### When agencies adopt EJ language, environmental groups do better

:::: {.columns}  

::: {.column width="20%"}


Within-organization (DiD) OLS model 

(N = 3,932 organizations)

:::

::: {.column width="80%"}

```{r, out.width = "100%"}

here("ej", 
     "docs",
     "figs", 
     "mo1ej-obama.png") |> 
  str_remove(d) |> 
knitr::include_graphics()

```
:::

::::

---

## Which environmental groups? 

```{r substantive, out.width = "85%", fig.show = "hold"}
here("ej", 
     "docs",
     "figs", 
     "ej-success-table.png") |> 
  str_remove(d) |> 
knitr::include_graphics()
```



```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
#| label: "tbl-ej-winrates"
#| tbl-cap: "Hand-coded Lobbying Success by Type of Organization, 2005-2024"

here("ej", 
     "data",
     "winrate.Rdata") |> 
  str_remove(d) |> 
  load()
     
winrates |> 
  dplyr::mutate(`EJ Success Rate` = `EJ Success Rate` |>
           scales::percent(accuracy = 1) |>
             tidyr::replace_na("-"),
         `Overall Success Rate` = `Overall Success Rate` |>
           scales::percent(accuracy = 1)) |> 
  dplyr::select(-`N raising EJ`) |>
  # name to nest
  dplyr::mutate(`Organization Type` = `Organization Type` |> 
           stringr::str_replace("(State|Tribe|City)", "Government, \\1") |> 
           stringr::str_replace("(Religious|Pressure Group|Professional Assn.|University|Environmental Group|Credit Union|Union|Frontline EJ)", "NGO, \\1") |> 
           stringr::str_replace("(Finance|Law Firm)", "Business, \\1") |> 
           stringr::str_replace("(NGO$|Business$)", "\\1 (Overall)")) |> 
dplyr::arrange(`Organization Type`) |> 
  knitr::kable()
```


# **How**: Responsiveness is about capacity and procedures

###  Little variation in **responsiveness** to comments

```{r}
#| label: fig-ej-m-PR-pres
#| fig-cap: "Responsiveness to Pressure Address Environmental Justice where the Proposed Rule Did Not Address Environmental Justice"
#| out-width: 100%

here("ej", 
     "docs",
     "figs", 
     "ej-m-PR-president-median-pres-2.png") |> 
  str_remove(d) |> 
knitr::include_graphics()
```


## Implications: 

Design processes for group participation. 

- give policymakers information on groups

If you want people to be heard,

- connect those people with groups
- connect those groups with each other and/or give those groups resources

If you want agencies to be responsive

- make it someone's job

Organization is the mobilization of bias. You cannot design institutions to be responsive *in general* to all things. They are responsive to the things they are tasked with doing.  


# Extra

##  Racialized Language  {#terms}

```{r}
here::here("data", "rules_terms.rda") |>
  stringr::str_replace("pressure-book", "racialization") |> 
  load()
```


> `r terms |> paste0(collapse = "; ")`


## 


:::: {.columns}  

::: {.column width="30%"}

### When agencies adopt EJ language, environmental coalitions do better 

Cross-sectional OLS model of coalition-level lobbying success (N = 284)

:::

::: {.column width="70%"}
```{r, out.width = "100%"}
here("ej", 
     "docs",
     "figs", 
     "mc1ej-obama.png") |> 
  str_remove(d) |> 
knitr::include_graphics()
```
:::

::::


## Agency rulemaking tilts toward the rich

```{r}
here("FINREGRULEMAKE2", 
     "finreg", 
     "figs", 
     "compustat-density-1.png") |> 
  str_remove(d) |> 
  knitr::include_graphics()
```


## LLMs
